data.title: Add searchable UI/UX design knowledge base as a reference library
data.description:
Add a structured, queryable design knowledge base that agents can consult during UI implementation and review — similar to ui-ux-pro-max-skill's searchable corpus.

## Reference: ui-ux-pro-max-skill

ui-ux-pro-max-skill (57k stars) ships a searchable design knowledge base with BM25 + regex hybrid ranking containing:
- 67 UI styles (Minimalism, Swiss Grid, Glassmorphism, etc.) with technical implementation details, psychological associations, and industry-appropriateness rules
- 161 color palettes aligned 1:1 with specific product types/industries
- 57 font pairings with personality matching rationale
- 25 chart type recommendations for dashboards/analytics
- 99 UX guidelines organized by priority rank 1-10
- 161 product types with recommended landing page patterns
- 24 landing page structures by conversion strategy
- 15 tech stacks with implementation guidance

For government digital services, this should include:
- USWDS (U.S. Web Design System) component patterns and usage guidance
- Government-specific anti-pattern catalog (no third-party tracking without consent, no smartphone-only auth, etc.)
- Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA compliance patterns per component type
- Approved icon libraries and typography for government contexts

This maps to existing epic e471-dda3 (reference libraries) as a UI/UX-specific reference library.
